summary: Strip junk boilerplate/apparatus from corpus chunks to fix retrieval mis-hits
description:
Stop boilerplate/apparatus text from polluting chunk embeddings and causing query mis-hits. Source analysis: docs/upstream-data-cleanup.md (mined from the live v27 corpus; corroborated on the local 3149-chunk corpus).

Evidence (local): 971 chunks (30%) carry a leading "Sacred Texts ... Previous Next" nav prefix — neoplatonism=716 (plotinus-select-works-index), taoism=137, egyptian=68, zoroastrianism=19, christian_mysticism=14, greek_mystery=10, jewish_mysticism=4, hermeticism=2, buddhism=1; 121 chunks carry {p. N} brace scan markers; 5 are pure ^Next:/^Previous: apparatus.

Root cause: most chunking/*/*.toml never got pre_strip_patterns. The mechanism already exists (scripts/chunk.py::_apply_pre_strip) and is used by orphic-hymns + mandaean; it just needs applying to the rest. (Alternative considered — doc caveat #4: strip at the source-HTML/downloader level, cleaner but more work; pre_strip_patterns on the configs is the pragmatic lever this plan uses.)

Hard dependency (doc caveat #3): stripping changes chunk text, so affected chunks MUST be re-chunked + re-embedded (their vectors were computed on the polluted text) and re-exported to guru-web PG — otherwise retrieval stays broken.

Global caveats applied to every child:
- Never drop by length alone — "Jesus said, 'Become passers-by.'" is a real 9-token Gospel-of-Thomas logion. Short != junk.
- Only the {p. N} brace form is an unambiguous scan artifact; bare "p. N" can be a legit citation — keep it.

Children (ordered): C1 nav-prefix strip (the ~30% 80/20) · C2 {p.N} brace + plate/figure strip · C3 drop pure-apparatus chunks · C4 structural (leading title/byline + interleaved footnotes) · C5 re-export to guru-web PG + verify mis-hits resolved.

resolution.note: Junk-text cleanup epic complete: C1 nav (#22), C2 braces/Buy (#24), C3 apparatus-drop + C4 byline (#25), all merged. Prod refresh by user.
